_purpose: All 40 pre-staged 2026ARK-AB candidates (from candidate_expansion_targets.txt) verified via live RCSB GraphQL metadata (initial_release_date + polymer_entity pdbx_description/auth_asym_ids), to expand Stage 3's N=12 abag pilot beyond its sampling-noise floor. NOT folded yet -- this is a verified, ready-to-use target list for whenever there is spare device time, gated behind finishing the core 12-target x 3-model OpenDDE self-consistency measurement first.
_method: For each candidate: (1) release date via RCSB (all confirmed 2026-01 through 2026-04, consistent with the original 12); (2) auth_asym_ids per polymer entity to detect multi-copy ASUs (same failure mode fixed for 9obn in Stage 0, see dockq-multicopy-chain-mapper-false-zero) vs genuine multi-interface/multi-chain-antigen structures; (3) pdbx_description read to exclude cases where the 'antigen' is itself an antibody-derived scaffold (the 9yxd exclusion pattern) or an unrelated fusion/adaptor protein reached only via a crystallization-chaperone antibody with no direct real antigen.

tier_a_clean_ready.description: Single copy, single real Ab-Ag interface. All 11 below now have committed, ready-to-fold yaml + ground-truth CIF (examples/abag_pilot_expansion/<pdb>_abag.yaml, examples/ground_truth_structures/<pdb>.cif), built from the REAL RESOLVED per-chain sequence extracted via gemmi from the actual RCSB CIF -- not the RCSB polymer_entity canonical/construct sequence, which caught a real discrepancy for 21tw (see yaml_prep_correction below) and would have silently fed 2 extra unresolved nanobody domains into the model input.

targets.note: already deeply characterized as an opendde-abag hard target, DockQ~0.011 both reference and device -- see opendde-9dsg-reference-parity-confirmed-not-port-bug; still a valid independent N point, expected fail case
same antigen as 9lof/9log -- 3 different antibodies vs the same viral protein, not fully independent samples statistically
ternary complex, ACE2 also present -- folded as the full 4-chain complex (chain id R = ACE2) for biological accuracy, score only the Fab(H/L)-S1(A) interface
resolved via RCSB struct.title: 9lp1 is the KP.3.1.1 RBD variant, 9loz (below) is the JN.1 RBD variant -- same antibody, genuinely DIFFERENT antigen sequences, NOT a duplicate. Both included.

targets.antigen: Spike protein S1 (SARS-CoV-2 JN.1 RBD variant)
targets.note: same antibody as 9lp1, different RBD variant (JN.1 vs KP.3.1.1) -- confirmed independent via RCSB struct.title, not a duplicate

tier_a_clean_ready.yaml_prep_correction: Fetching resolved sequences (gemmi on the real CIF) instead of trusting RCSB's polymer_entity canonical sequence caught 2 real issues before they reached a yaml: (1) 21tw's nanobody entity reads as 363 residues in RCSB's polymer_entity record (the full trivalent Ozoralizumab-derived expression construct: 2x anti-TNF + 1x anti-HSA domain), but only 224 residues (a single VHH domain) are actually resolved in the crystal and belong in the fold input -- using the full 363mer would have fed 2 irrelevant unresolved binding domains into the model. (2) 9gei and 9hv9 were reclassified out of Tier A after the real-CIF check (see below) rather than folded with wrong assumptions.

tier_peptide_epitope_different_category.description: Real target, real antibody, but the antigen chain resolved in the crystal is a short LINEAR PEPTIDE (not a folded protein domain) -- a qualitatively different prediction problem (epitope/peptide docking, not rigid-domain Ab-Ag docking) from the rest of this benchmark. Not folded as a Tier A target; flagged separately so it isn't silently averaged into the same precision-at-coverage numbers as domain-antigen targets.

targets.note: RCSB's polymer_entity record describes the antigen as the full 'Capsid protein VP1', but gemmi on the real CIF shows only 7 residues (KIVAPVK) are actually resolved for that chain -- a linear peptide epitope, not the folded VP1 domain. Caught by the same resolved-sequence check that fixed 21tw's yaml.

tier_b_needs_multicopy_trim.description: Real single interface but 2 crystallographic copies of the complex in the ASU -- needs the same gemmi CA-CA distance trim to the one real contacting triple that Stage 0 applied to 9obn before scoring.

targets.note: also possibly Tier D (raised for GPCR structure determination) -- do not assume therapeutic intent

moved here from an earlier Tier A pass -- RCSB's entity record only listed 1 chain per role, but the real CIF has 2 full copies (A/B and C/D), a multi-copy ASU this pass's first check missed until it fetched auth_asym_ids for the actual structure

tier_c_complex_multichain.description: 2 distinct real Ab-Ag interfaces in one entry, or a multi-chain/multi-domain real antigen -- usable but needs chain-subsetting work before folding, not a drop-in extension of the existing yaml pattern.

targets.note: two different antibodies (BD70-4008, BD70-4017) both bound to the same Envelopment-polyprotein antigen chain -- usable as 2 separate N points

targets.antigen: gp42 + envelope glycoprotein H + L (EBV entry complex, 3-chain antigen)

targets.note: real antigen is beta-arrestin, not the GPCR mega-complex it's embedded in (PTH1R + LA-PTH peptide + Fab30 + arrestin) -- would need to subset just the Fab30-arrestin chains
near-duplicate of 9lxp (same Fab30-arrestin pair, different GPCR partner in the mega-complex) -- check for redundancy before folding both

tier_d_chaperone_style.description: Real, distinct, non-antibody protein antigen, but the antibody fragment was very likely raised/selected as a structural-biology crystallization aid (nanobody/scFv fiducial for a GPCR or transporter) rather than a therapeutic candidate. Included with this caveat, not excluded -- the model still has to solve the same 3D docking problem regardless of why the antibody exists.

excluded.description: Real disqualifying issue, not just caution.

targets.reason: the 'antibody' is a ProMacrobody scaffold and the two candidate antigens are Grb2/THEMIS adaptor proteins -- reads as a chaperone-aided protein-protein-interaction structure, not a clean single antibody-vs-one-antigen case
double indirect: the Fab's actual epitope is a BRIL fusion tag, not the real GPCR target, and the second 'antibody' present is an anti-Fab nanobody targeting the Fab itself, not an antigen
MHC-class-I-peptide-restricted 'TCR-mimic' antibody (Fab M1/42 binds a peptide-MHC-beta2m complex, not a normal protein antigen) -- atypical antigen type, excluded from this pass's scope
same MHC-peptide-restricted pattern as 9d72 (B1.23.2 mAb vs HLA-B*44:05 + peptide + beta2m)
same MHC-peptide-restricted pattern (mouse mAb 11-72 vs H-2 class II MHC + lysozyme peptide)

unclear_not_classified.description: Metadata insufficient to classify confidently this pass -- flagged, not fabricated as checked.

targets.reason: entities are 'Heavy chain'/'Light chain'/'gamma chain'/'TRA@ protein' -- ambiguous whether this is a normal Ab-Ag pair or an Fc-receptor/TCR-related complex; needs a closer read before use

summary_counts.note_on_total: all 40 pre-staged candidates checked; 9lp1 and 9loz confirmed genuinely distinct (different RBD variants of the same antibody), not a duplicate -- both included in Tier A
